senses.gloss: to furnish with a tail, to provide a start to

examples.text: رَبَّنَا فَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَكَفِّرْ عَنَّا سَيِّئَاتِنَا وَتَوَفَّنَا مَعَ الْأَبْرَارِ
examples.english: Our Lord! Lo! we have heard a crier calling unto Faith: "Believe ye in your Lord!" So we believed. Our Lord! Therefor forgive us our sins, and remit from us our evil deeds, and make us die the death of the righteous.

examples.text: وَإِذَا ٱلۡمَوۡءُودَةُ سُئِلَتۡ ۝ بِأَيِّ ذَنۢبࣲ قُتِلَتۡ ۝
examples.english: And when the girl-child that was buried alive is asked. 9. For what sin she was slain

etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Proto-Semitic *ḏanab-
Arabic ذنب
Inherited from Proto-Semitic *ḏanab-.

body.text: A sin, a crime, a fault, a misdemeanour, a misdeed, an unlawful deed, an offence, a transgression,
either intentional or committed through inadvertence;
a thing that precludes one from
a thing for which he is blamable who does it intentionally:
[in the Kur xxvi. 13, said by Moses, meaning
] refers to the speaker's slaughter of him whom he struck, who was of the family of Pharaoh. (M.)
